function_explanation: Evaluates the accuracy of a diffusion model fit by comparing estimated parameters against ground truth values and calculating the Mean Squared Error (MSE) of the reconstructed signal.
function_docstring:
Compares fitted parameters with ground truth and calculates reconstruction error.

Args:
    fitted_params: Array of shape (5,) containing the estimated model parameters
        [f_stick, theta, phi, lambda_par, lambda_iso].
    gt_params: Array of shape (5,) containing the ground truth model parameters
        corresponding to the fitted parameters.
    bvalues: Array of shape (N,) representing the diffusion weighting factors.
    gradient_directions: Array of shape (N, 3) representing the normalized gradient
        direction vectors.
    data: Array of shape (N,) containing the observed diffusion signal attenuation.

Returns:
    float: The Mean Squared Error (MSE) between the observed data and the signal
        reconstructed using the fitted parameters.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Define mock parameters (f_stick, theta, phi, lambda_par, lambda_iso)
fitted_params = np.array([0.5, 1.57, 0.0, 1.7e-9, 0.8e-9])
gt_params = np.array([0.52, 1.60, 0.1, 1.75e-9, 0.82e-9])

# Define acquisition scheme
N_samples = 10
bvalues = np.full(N_samples, 1000.0)
gradient_directions = np.random.randn(N_samples, 3)
gradient_directions /= np.linalg.norm(gradient_directions, axis=1, keepdims=True)

# Define observed data
data = np.random.rand(N_samples)

# Evaluate results
mse = evaluate_results(fitted_params, gt_params, bvalues, gradient_directions, data)
print(f"Calculated MSE: {mse}")
